Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to recall what a normative Hire-to-Retire process should look like From the description it should be a linear sequence post job ad screen candidates interview make decision onboard add to payroll close case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Model 1 first The code shows a strict partial order with edges Post Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the anomaly here is that Interview happens after Screen but theres no edge from Interview to Decide In a standard process youd screen candidates then interview them then make a decision But here Decide comes directly after Screen without Interview being a prerequisite That means someone could make a hiring decision without ever conducting interviews

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Payroll is mandatory no choice structure which is correct since you must add new hires to payroll before closing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Model 2 is more complex It has a loop for onboarding either Onboard or skip XOR for payroll Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model allows two parallel paths after Post one through Screen and one through Interview Then both converge at Decide screening should happen before interviewing Having them as parallel branches means you could interview candidates who havent been screened The loop onboarding means onboarding could be skipped entirely xor payroll allows skipping payroll addition

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparing the two models Model 1s main flaw is missing Interview Decide dependency Model 2 has multiple severe flaws parallel screening interviewing optional onboarding optional payroll addition

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 at least has the correct sequence from Post to Close with all activities mandatory just with a logical gap Model 2 has fundamental structural issues where critical steps can be skipped and the process flow is illogical Therefore Model 1 is closer to normative despite its flaw because Model 2 violates more fundamental principles

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: I should structure my response to 1 Define normative process clearly 2 Analyze Model 1 anomalies 3 Analyze Model 2 anomalies 4 Compare severity and conclude Model 1 is better 5 Justify why Model 1s anomaly is less damaging than Model 2s multiple flaws
